title: Hesta loads up amid turmoil
full_text:
Industry superannuation fund Hesta has quietly been buying hundreds of thousands of shares in WiseTech Global, despite publicly threatening to dump its stake in the troubled logistics software player over the scandals surrounding founder Richard White.
The $100bn fund has been the most vocal about WiseTech’s governance woes and has demanded it “confront the uncertainty” around its leadership.
The fund intensified its demands over WiseTech after billionaire Mr White, the executive chairman, became the target of a second live criminal investigation.
Hesta chief Debby Blakey this week lashed the company, ordering it to “explain the allegations” which involve sex exploitation and reveal how WiseTech will “manage” Mr White. He denies any involvement in sex trafficking.
Hesta put WiseTech on its watchlist in May 2025, warning it would dump its entire stake unless the company got its governance in order. But as ASIC launched a separate investigation into employees, including Mr White, for alleged insider trading last October, Hesta quietly bought more than 680,700 WiseTech shares through the December quarter, according to latest company filings.
